        blogPost: `
Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!\n\n
1. Behind-the-Scenes:\n
Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.\n\n
2. User-Generated Content (UGC):\n
Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.\n\n
3. Inspirational Quotes:\n
Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.\n\n
4. Product or Service Highlights:\n
Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.\n\n
5. Tutorials and How-Tos:\n
Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche.\n\n
6. Customer Testimonials:\n
Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you.\n\n
7. Polls and Surveys:\n
Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers.\n\n
8. Throwback Posts:\n
Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.\n\n
9. Seasonal Content:\n
Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.\n\n
10. Fun Facts and Trivia:\n
Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more.\n\n
11. Collaborations and Partnerships:\n
Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.\n\n
12. Company Culture:\n
Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives.\n\n
Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!`

content.text:
Storybook now mirrors the app’s real runtime instead of the starter scaffold. In [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/.storybook/preview.tsx#L1) I copied the real app CSS import, wrapped stories in router context, seeded the same `sessionStorage` user shape the app expects, stubbed `fetch` for the captcha and Airtable endpoints, and stabilized browser APIs like `scrollTo`, `navigator.share`, and `navigator.clipboard`. In [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/.storybook/main.ts#L1) I added `public` as a static dir, injected deterministic `VITE_*` values, and aliased ReCAPTCHA to [`.storybook/mocks/GoogleReCaptchaMock.jsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/.storybook/mocks/GoogleReCaptchaMock.jsx). I also added [`tsconfig.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/tsconfig.json) so the new preview and TSX stories typecheck cleanly.

I replaced the generic starter stories with thin colocated stories copied from real usage patterns, including [Navigation](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/components/navigation/Navigation.stories.tsx), [Landing](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/landing/Landing.stories.tsx), [Pricing](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/pricing/Pricing.stories.tsx), [Blog](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/blog/Blog.stories.tsx), [Contact](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/contact/Contact.stories.tsx), [SignIn](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/sign-in/SignIn.stories.tsx), [SignUp](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/sign-up/SignUp.stories.tsx), and [Profile](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/src/routes/user-profile/Profile.stories.tsx). I removed the old `src/stories` starter stories so Storybook only shows app-backed stories.

Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `STORYBOOK_FAIL_ON_EMPTY_RENDER=true STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS=true npx vitest --project storybook ...`; both passed, with 9 story files and 12 Storybook tests green. The pre-existing untracked [`.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-9e298f2b/project/.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md) was left untouched.
